chapter_context.summary: Exalts God's control of the king's heart, prefers justice to sacrifice, warns against contentious spouses, and declares that ultimate victory comes from the Lord.

outline.text: King's Heart in LORD's Hand, Justice Preferred, and Haughty Eyes
outline.explanation: This section focuses on King's Heart in LORD's Hand, Justice Preferred, and Haughty Eyes (21:1-8), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Proverbs.

outline.text: Dwelling in Corner, Wicked's Desire, and Cry of the Poor
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Dwelling in Corner, Wicked's Desire, and Cry of the Poor (21:9-16), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Proverbs.

outline.text: Loving Pleasure, Righteous Ransom, and Contentious Woman
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Loving Pleasure, Righteous Ransom, and Contentious Woman (21:17-24), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Proverbs.

outline.text: Sluggard's Covetousness, Wicked Sacrifices, and Victory of LORD
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Sluggard's Covetousness, Wicked Sacrifices, and Victory of LORD (21:25-31), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Proverbs.

key_themes.title: Wisdom vs. Folly
key_themes.description: Proverbs outlines a practical blueprint for daily living, contrasting the actions, words, and consequences of the wise with those of the foolish.

key_themes.title: The Fear of the Lord
key_themes.description: The foundational principle of wisdom is a reverent awe and trust in God, which guides moral choices and shapes character.

devotional.question: Reflecting on the practical sayings of Chapter 21, what is one specific area of your speech, relationships, or work where you can apply divine wisdom today?
How does this chapter prompt you to choose the path of integrity and diligence over convenience or compromise?

cultural_historical.title: Ancient Near Eastern Wisdom Literature
cultural_historical.text: Proverbs shares structural and thematic similarities with other ancient wisdom writings, but stands unique in placing the Fear of Yahweh as the foundation of all true wisdom.
